intent:
PREMISE CONTRACT (story 12, final — round 4 R1 absorption)
  one_sentence_event:    The teacher gave the narrator the teacher's own long, still-warm pencil because the narrator's pencil was short; at home the narrator placed it carefully and shared tea with mother.
  change_of_state:       at s0 narrator has only a short worn pencil. at the closer narrator is at home with the teacher's warm pencil resting on a book and is sharing tea with mother. Possession + identity-of-object + scene + relational-warmth all change.
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a kitchen / phone_call / rooftop story. Scene = classroom (first appearance in v2 corpus); home is brief coda.
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is 鉛筆 (pencil). Never used as anchor before. Last appeared as ★ debt word in story 4. Six occurrences spanning s0, s1, s2, s3, s4, s5 — held, inspected by teacher, gifted, identified, felt-warm, placed at home.
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: noun-WA i-adj-PAST-です — 「お茶はおいしかったです。」 Acknowledged risk: surface mirrors story 11's quoted question 「お茶はおいしかったですか。」 (different speaker, different modality — declarative vs interrogative). Justified by R1 reinforcement obligation that requires W00082 (おいしかった) be reused; this is the only natural sentence at N5 that lands the past-tense form on tea.
  why_not_filler:        s0 deficit. s1 introduces teacher + her physical approach (来る) + her noticing (見) — three beats compressed for sentence-budget. s2 IS the gift (そして + くれる). s3 reveals it was the teacher's own (relational reveal). s4 sensory confirmation (warm). s5 home-coda compresses three actions: 行く (movement), 置く (placing the gift safely), あげる (sharing tea with mother — gratitude flows outward). s6 closer — the tea was good (relational warmth circle).
  obligations_absorbed:  must_reinforce N5_i_adj_past = TRIPLE landing (s0 短かった, s4 温かかった, s6 おいしかった). must_introduce N5_soshite at s2. R1 reinforcements (forced by post-ship pytest sweep that flagged story 11's W00079-83 with 0 follow-ups): W00079 行く @ s5; W00080 置く @ s5; W00081 来る @ s1; W00082 おいしかった @ s6; W00083 あげる @ s5. ALL FIVE story-11 mints reinforced — R1 cleared.

GRAMMAR DEFERRAL: brief recommended N4_passive (priority 6). Deferred — passive forces unnatural pedagogical phrasing in a week-4 N5 reader. Chose N5_soshite (clause-level connector) instead.

LEXICAL: 4 mints — 短い, 教室, 長い, くれる. All N5 / top-frequency. No overrides.

OVERRIDE USED (1/1): §G override token. The R1 absorption coda forces a structurally heavier story than the original literary version (round 3) and a closer that surface-mirrors story 11. Justified by: post-ship pytest must be green; story 11 left an unsolvable R1 debt (5 new words, only 1 followup window); deferring to story 13 leaves the test red across sessions. The 7-sentence compressed form preserves the relational reveal (s3) and sensory beat (s4) while folding the home actions into one chained-te-form sentence.

LITERARY-GATE NOTES (truncated — full reasoning in earlier rounds):
  Round 1 §E.7: REWRITE-STORY (transactional). Round 2 §E.7: REWRITE-STORY (bolt-on s3, post-hoc reveal). Round 3 §E.7: REWRITE-STORY (closer = action not experience). §E.7.5 SHIPPED at 7/7 in round 3. R1 cascade forced round 4 restructure.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY:
  scene_class    (last 3): kitchen, phone_call, rooftop
  anchor_object  (last 5): towel, 卵, 本, 雲, 電話
  closer_shape   (last 3): P:でも-noun-WA-noun-NI-noun, noun-NO-noun-WO-noun-NI-noun-DESU, noun-TO-noun-WA-noun-NI-noun-NO-noun-NI-noun
  opening_seqs   (last 3): 今日/友達/学校, 夕方/母/私, 朝/台所/行きます

sentences.jp: 私の鉛筆は短かったです。
教室に先生がいました。
sentences.en: There was a teacher in the classroom.

sentences.jp: 先生が私のところに来て、私の鉛筆を見ました。
sentences.en: The teacher came over to me, and looked at my pencil.

sentences.jp: そして、先生は長い鉛筆をくれました。
sentences.en: And then the teacher gave me a long pencil.

sentences.jp: その鉛筆は先生のでした。
sentences.en: That pencil was the teacher's own.

sentences.jp: 鉛筆は温かかったです。
ゆっくり家に行って、鉛筆を本に置いて、母にお茶をあげました。
sentences.en: I walked home slowly, placed the pencil on a book, and gave Mother some tea.

sentences.jp: お茶はおいしかったです。
